message:
test(proptest): add property-based tests for runtime_compat dual-runtime abstraction

New file: tests/proptest_runtime_compat.rs (873 lines, ~45 properties)

Systematically verifies the invariants of every public primitive in the
runtime_compat module — the abstraction layer that bridges tokio and
asupersync runtimes. All tests use a with_tokio() helper that creates a
current-thread runtime per test case, ensuring proptest compatibility.

MPSC channel properties (200 cases each):
  - FIFO ordering: messages arrive in exact send order for arbitrary i64 seqs
  - Completeness: recv count == send count for arbitrary u32 sequences
  - Closed-channel error: mpsc_send to dropped receiver returns SendError
    containing the original value
  - Drain termination: mpsc_recv_option returns None after sender drop + drain
  - String roundtrip: String values survive send→recv cycle intact

Watch channel properties (200 cases each):
  - Latest-value semantics: borrow() always returns most recently sent value
  - Initial value preservation: borrow() before any send returns constructor arg
  - Cloned receiver convergence: cloned rx instances see identical values
  - No-receiver error: send after all receivers dropped returns Err

Broadcast channel properties (150 cases each):
  - FIFO ordering per receiver: messages arrive in send order
  - Fan-out consistency: 3 independent receivers all see identical message seqs
  - No-receiver error: send with no live receivers fails

Semaphore permit accounting (200 cases each):
  - Initial permits match constructor argument
  - try_acquire decrements available_permits by exactly 1 per call
  - Dropping all permits restores original count (conservation law)
  - Exhausted semaphore: try_acquire after all permits held returns error
  - Closed semaphore rejects all subsequent try_acquire calls
  - Partial acquire then close: held permits survive, new acquires blocked

Mutex data integrity (200 cases each):
  - Last-write-wins: sequential writes leave final value
  - Accumulation correctness: summing i64 sequence under mutex matches expected
  - Vec integrity: push sequence preserved through lock/unlock cycles
  - Initial value preserved when no writes occur

RwLock data integrity (200 cases each):
  - Write-then-read returns written value
  - Last-write-wins across write sequence
  - Accumulation correctness via write guard
  - Initial value preserved with read-only access
  - Vec integrity through write guard push + read guard verify

RuntimeBuilder properties (100 cases each):
  - current_thread() always builds successfully
  - multi_thread() always builds successfully
  - multi_thread().worker_threads(1..8) always builds
  - current_thread() silently ignores worker_threads (no panic)
  - block_on preserves arbitrary i64 computation results
  - Nested async within block_on preserves both values

Timeout properties (100 cases each):
  - Immediate futures never timeout regardless of duration
  - Complex return types (Vec<u8>) preserved through timeout wrapper
  - Result<T, E> types preserved through timeout wrapper

spawn_blocking properties (100 cases each):
  - Closure result roundtrip for arbitrary i64 values
  - Arithmetic computation correctness (a*b + a + b)
  - String value roundtrip through blocking thread
  - Vec construction on blocking thread preserves all elements

Sleep properties (30 cases each):
  - Any small duration (0-50ms) completes without panic
  - Zero duration completes in <100ms

Cross-primitive integration (50 cases each):
  - Mutex + mpsc: protected counter accumulation matches channel message sum
  - Semaphore + Mutex: bounded-access counter equals iteration count,
    all permits restored after completion
  - Runtime + channel + Mutex: full workflow (send→recv→accumulate) preserves
    value ordering through block_on

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
